excerpt: Indeed, our Shi'a are those who believe in Allah and His Messenger...Our Shi'a are those who observe the heavens...He is not of our Shi'a who seizes the rights of others
source_location_hint: Sermon on the characteristics of the Shi'a

excerpt: I counsel you all to fear Allah and obey His commands...When you follow the commandments that We have enjoined upon you...then people will say these are followers of such and such Imam
source_location_hint: On the characteristics required of the Shi'a

excerpt: Whoever Allah tests by putting piety in the heart of a believer...shall surely love us with sincere heart...Whoever doubts us and joins our enemies is not of us
source_location_hint: Sermon on love and enmity toward the Imams

excerpt: O Shi'a, listen and understand our will...Speak truth and fulfill your oaths...Our Shi'a are those who follow us, not those who oppose us. When we are afraid, they too are afraid; when we live in peace, they live in tranquility
source_location_hint: Teachings on the conduct and characteristics of the Shi'a

excerpt: O Hammām, fear Allah and do righteous deeds, for Allah is with those who fear Him and those who do good. [Series of questions and responses about piety]
source_location_hint: Sermon 193: Description of the God-fearing to Hammām ibn Sharīḥ

excerpt: If their soul desires a righteous deed, they do not allow it to act upon caprice...They transform their knowledge into patience, and their words into deeds...They are dignified in adversity, patient in hardship, and grateful in days of ease
source_location_hint: Sermon 193: Continued description of attributes of the pious

excerpt: When the Prophet made people testify to his prophethood, he first expressed himself through servanthood and only then announced himself as messenger...Allah was so pleased with this humility that when He mentioned the Night Journey, He employed the word messenger rather than servant
source_authority: Commentary on Quranic verses on servitude
source_location_hint: Discussion of the ranks of servitude and messengership

excerpt: The love of the Messenger of Allah is such a magnet, endowed with such blessings, that Allah forgives the sins of His servants for the sake of their love for His Messenger
source_location_hint: Commentary on love and obedience to the Prophet

excerpt: Allah the Almighty has founded His religion upon love, which is called the Law of Attraction (qānūn al-alf). Love is the act of accepting another's will, desire, and intention for the sake of that other's will
source_authority: Cosmological principles of the Law of Attraction
source_location_hint: On the universal law governing divine order

excerpt: If anyone desires to know whether he is our friend or our foe, let him test his heart. If he loves one of our friends, he is not our enemy. If he harbors enmity toward one of our friends, he is not our friend
source_authority: The Prophet (ṣallā Allāhu ʿalayhi wa-ālihi wa-sallam)
source_location_hint: On the criterion for recognizing friends and enemies of the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: You shall never attain our wilāya except through piety, through striving, through truthful speech, through rendering trusts...Even if the murderer of Amir al-Mu'minin ʿAlī entrusted me with a trust, I would return it to him
source_location_hint: On the conditions for attaining wilāya of the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: No servant can believe until I am dearer to him than his own self, my progeny dearer to him than his own progeny, my family dearer to him than his own family, and my person dearer to him than his own person
source_authority: The Prophet (ṣallā Allāhu ʿalayhi wa-ālihi wa-sallam)
source_location_hint: On the perfection of faith through love of the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: Allah has not placed two hearts within a man's breast...Whoever wishes to know whether he loves us, let him test his heart. If in his love for us he shares the love of our enemy, he is not of us
source_location_hint: On the purity and exclusivity of love for the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: During my Night Journey, an angel came to me and said, O Muhammad, ask the Messengers who were sent before you...They replied, We called them to your wilāya and to the wilāya of ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_authority: The Prophet (ṣallā Allāhu ʿalayhi wa-ālihi wa-sallam)
source_location_hint: On the centrality of wilāya throughout prophetic tradition

excerpt: Faith is not perfected except through the love of us, the Ahl al-Bayt. Indeed, it is the covenant of Allah with me that none shall love the Ahl al-Bayt except the pious believer, and none shall hate us except the hardened hypocrite
source_authority: The Prophet (ṣallā Allāhu ʿalayhi wa-ālihi wa-sallam)
source_location_hint: On the necessity of love for the Ahl al-Bayt for the perfection of faith

excerpt: The remembrance of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is worship. My remembrance is worship. The remembrance of ʿAlī is worship. The remembrance of the Imams from among my progeny is worship
source_authority: The Prophet (ṣallā Allāhu ʿalayhi wa-ālihi wa-sallam)
source_location_hint: On the spiritual rank and merit of remembering the Prophet and the Imams

excerpt: O you who believe! Whosoever of you turns back from his religion, Allah will bring a people whom Allah desires and who love Him, gentle with the believers, mighty against the disbelievers
source_location_hint: Verse 5:54

excerpt: Say, if you love God, then follow me, and God will love you and forgive you your sins
source_location_hint: Verse 3:31

excerpt: One of the seventy thousand parts of our love, knowing that he would not be able to bear it
source_location_hint: Anecdote on divine love

excerpt: O Shu'aib, you are indeed My beloved
source_location_hint: Story of Shu'aib and divine love

excerpt: If you love God, then follow me, and God will love you
source_location_hint: Verse 3:31

excerpt: One day this child will be sacrificed for my Hussein
source_work: Narrative tradition on Habib ibn Mazahir
source_location_hint: Hadith on Habib's destiny

excerpt: He used to finish the entire Qur'an in one night
source_location_hint: Character description of Habib

excerpt: Allah has founded His religion in accordance with His creation, so that creation itself becomes proof of His religion
source_location_hint: Teachings on harmony between creation and Shariah

excerpt: I am the city of knowledge and Ali is its gate
source_location_hint: Saying of the Prophet regarding Ali

excerpt: Enter the gate bowing, and speak with humility saying Hatta (forgiveness)
source_authority: Quranic reference to Children of Israel
source_location_hint: Quranic narrative (2:58)

excerpt: This day have I perfected your religion for you and completed My favor upon you
source_location_hint: Verse 5:3 on the Day of Ghadir

excerpt: The absence of wilayat will render all practices useless and they will revert to the religion of ignorance
source_location_hint: Interpretation of role of wilayat

excerpt: If he does not acquire knowledge of his Imam of the time, he will inevitably become ignorant of the sciences of his religion
source_location_hint: On knowledge of the Imam

excerpt: The greater a person's knowledge, the greater his fear of Allah
source_location_hint: On fear and knowledge

excerpt: Do you not know that this world is a prison for the believer
source_location_hint: Teaching on detachment from the world

excerpt: The children of Adam reap what they sow in the field of their lives. No one obtains sweetness from bitterness, nor bitterness from sweetness
source_location_hint: On justice and consequences

excerpt: If you are grateful, I will give you much more
source_location_hint: Verse 14:7 on gratitude

excerpt: Allaah has established two reasons for all obligations, the combination of which is an obligatory obligation. Of these two causes, one is the former and the general, while the other is the latter and is special.
source_location_hint: Councils on obligations and their causes

excerpt: Thus, the first reason for having a nisab of wealth in zakat is the first reason, but it is not obligatory to pay zakat only on the completion of the wealth, rather it is obligatory to spend a whole year, which is the second reason, after which zakat is obligatory.
source_location_hint: Councils on zakat and its conditions

excerpt: In contrast to all this, 'wilayat' is an obligation that has no reason to exist. This obligation is obligatory in all circumstances from birth to death, and for this reason it is not related to the organs but to the heart, which is the possession of every human being.
source_location_hint: Councils on wilayat and its unique status
